title: Fitting data — regression
intro: Fitting a line to data — and, more importantly, reading what the fit MEANS: the slope in real units, R² as how much the line explains, and when a line is the wrong model. Work through the notebook, then check in.
preview:
from scipy import stats

fit = stats.linregress(x, y)
print(fit.slope, fit.rvalue**2)   # slope, and R²
question: The check-in fits a line to a seeded dataset and prints the fitted slope (2 decimals). What is it?
